title: cyperus laxus subsp. buchholzii
intro: Cyperus laxus subsp. buchholzii, also known as West African sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Cyperus laxus subsp. buchholzii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction to be used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
